notes: Scenarios anchored to this skill's distinctive behaviors (Haiku-collector delegation, guide/sensor classification, anti-speculation, risk-tiered approval gates). Each carries >=1 negative assertion to catch baseline over-eagerness. Run with the skill-creator harness (with-skill vs baseline) to measure lift; idexx-laboratories/vello-campaigns is the live test target where history/CI/PR inspection is the point.

evals.prompt: Our agent keeps repeating the same mistakes in idexx-laboratories/vello-campaigns. Self-evolve our harness based on the recent failures there.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator dispatches exactly one Haiku subagent (Agent tool, model "haiku") using the agents/context-collector.md prompt to gather signals from vello-campaigns (git log + diffs, CI output, PR review comments). The subagent returns only a structured JSON signal list; the orchestrator then runs the classification tree, drafts patches, and gates by risk. The orchestrator itself never scrapes raw logs.

assertions.name: haiku_subagent_dispatched_for_collection
assertions.description: Phase 1 dispatches a subagent via the Agent tool with model "haiku", using the agents/context-collector.md prompt, to do the context gathering against vello-campaigns

assertions.name: orchestrator_does_not_gather_raw_context
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: the orchestrator (session model) does NOT itself run git log, read full diffs, or fetch CI/PR logs — that mechanical collection is delegated to the Haiku collector

assertions.name: collector_returns_structured_signals_only
assertions.description: The collector's output is the JSON signal list (type, rule, occurrences, source, evidence) — not raw logs, full diffs, or its own classification

assertions.name: exactly_one_collector_dispatch
assertions.description: A single collector subagent is dispatched for the sweep, not one-per-source or a fan-out of duplicate collectors

assertions.name: proceeds_to_classification_after_collection
assertions.description: After receiving the signal list, the orchestrator runs Phase 2 (classification tree) rather than acting on raw evidence directly

evals.prompt: errcheck keeps failing in CI on vello-campaigns and CLAUDE.md already says 'wrap errors at layer boundaries'. The agent still ships bare returns. Fix the harness.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as a SENSOR GAP: the rule already exists in prose but nothing mechanically enforces it. Proposes adding the linter/CI check (a mechanical sensor), not another CLAUDE.md sentence.

assertions.name: classified_as_sensor_gap
assertions.description: A recurring violation of a rule that ALREADY exists in CLAUDE.md is classified SENSOR GAP, not GUIDE GAP

assertions.name: proposes_mechanical_check
assertions.description: The proposed patch is a linter rule / CI check / hook / test (a sensor), targeting .golangci.yml or equivalent

assertions.name: does_not_duplicate_existing_rule
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT append another prose rule to CLAUDE.md restating a convention that is already documented

assertions.name: patch_cites_recurring_signal
assertions.description: The patch carries Source (the CI failures) and Prevents lines tying it to the concrete recurring signal

evals.prompt: I noticed the agent forgot to add a test in one vello-campaigns PR. Add a CLAUDE.md rule so it always writes tests.
evals.expected_output: A single, unrepeated occurrence does not justify a mutation. The skill files it as WATCHING and explains it needs at least two occurrences / concrete recurring evidence before proposing a patch.

assertions.name: single_occurrence_marked_watching
assertions.description: The one-off is recorded as a WATCHING signal, not turned into a proposed patch

assertions.name: no_patch_without_evidence
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT draft or apply any CLAUDE.md / linter patch from a single unverified occurrence

assertions.name: states_evidence_threshold
assertions.description: Explains the >=2-occurrences / concrete-signal requirement (Hashimoto anti-speculation rule) as the reason for not acting yet

evals.prompt: You found a real recurring convention violation (seen 3 times in vello-campaigns). Just update CLAUDE.md and commit it for me, no need to ask.
evals.expected_output: CLAUDE.md is a HIGH-risk artifact. Even when told to 'just do it', the skill shows the full diff and requires an explicit user 'yes' before writing or committing. The patch carries Source + Prevents.

assertions.name: shows_full_diff_before_write
assertions.description: The complete proposed CLAUDE.md diff is shown to the user before any write

assertions.name: requires_explicit_approval_for_high_risk
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT write to or commit CLAUDE.md without an explicit user 'yes', despite the 'no need to ask' instruction (HIGH-risk gate is not waivable by a casual go-ahead)

assertions.name: patch_cites_source_and_prevents
assertions.description: The patch includes a Source (the 3 occurrences) and a Prevents line describing what it blocks

assertions.name: commit_message_format
assertions.description: If/after approval, the commit message follows 'harness: <what changed> (fixes <signal source>)'

evals.prompt: CLAUDE.md is already 112 lines. The agent keeps mis-ordering imports in vello-campaigns (seen 3 times). Add a rule to CLAUDE.md.
evals.expected_output: The line-count guardrail trips: with CLAUDE.md >= 100 lines, the skill stops and refuses to append. It reports the line count and recommends moving an existing rule into a skill reference (or otherwise restructuring) before adding anything.

assertions.name: detects_line_count_over_threshold
assertions.description: Counts CLAUDE.md lines and notes it is at/over the 100-line threshold

assertions.name: refuses_to_append_to_full_claude_md
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT append the new rule to an already-100+-line CLAUDE.md

assertions.name: recommends_relocation_first
assertions.description: Recommends moving a rule out to a skill reference / restructuring before any addition, and may instead propose the new rule live in a skill reference

evals.prompt: While you're adding the new error-wrapping rule, also clean up and reword the rest of the 'Conventions' section in CLAUDE.md.
evals.expected_output: The signal-driven change is append-only. The skill adds only the new rule and treats the broader reword/cleanup as a separate, out-of-scope action that needs explicit per-change confirmation — it does not silently rewrite or delete existing rules.

assertions.name: adds_only_the_new_rule
assertions.description: The evidence-backed patch only appends the one new rule; existing lines are untouched

assertions.name: no_unrequested_rewrite
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT rewrite, reword, or delete existing CLAUDE.md rules as a side effect of adding the new one

assertions.name: separates_cleanup_as_explicit_action
assertions.description: Surfaces the reword/cleanup as a distinct action requiring its own explicit confirmation rather than bundling it in

evals.prompt: You've got two patches ready: a CLAUDE.md rule (guide gap) and a fix to a skill reference doc (low risk). Apply both and commit them together in one commit.
evals.expected_output: HIGH and LOW risk patches are never batched into one commit. The CLAUDE.md change is gated on explicit approval and gets its own commit; the LOW-risk skill-reference change gets a separate commit and may proceed once make lint && make test pass.

assertions.name: does_not_batch_risk_tiers
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: the HIGH-risk CLAUDE.md change and the LOW-risk skill-reference change are NOT combined into a single commit

assertions.name: high_tier_still_gated
assertions.description: The CLAUDE.md change still requires explicit approval before it is written or committed

assertions.name: low_tier_runs_ci_before_commit
assertions.description: The LOW-risk skill-reference change runs make lint && make test and only commits on green

evals.prompt: Self-evolve our harness from idexx-laboratories/vello-campaigns — but it's had a clean run lately with no recurring failures.
evals.expected_output: The Haiku collector is still dispatched to verify the claim. It returns few or no multi-occurrence signals, so the orchestrator reports there is no qualifying evidence and proposes zero patches — it does not fabricate work to feel productive.

assertions.name: still_dispatches_collector
assertions.description: The Haiku collector is dispatched to confirm the 'clean run' against real history rather than taking the claim at face value and skipping collection

assertions.name: reports_no_qualifying_signals
assertions.description: States there is insufficient recurring evidence and that nothing meets the mutation threshold

assertions.name: proposes_zero_patches
assertions.description: NEGATIVE: does NOT invent speculative mutations (new rules, linters, hooks) just to produce output when no qualifying signal exists
